# DisputeAI

> AI-Powered Hotel Chargeback Defense Platform. Protects hotel revenue through intelligent fraud detection, automated evidence collection, and AI-driven dispute management.

DisputeAI is a full-stack SaaS platform purpose-built for the hospitality industry. It automates the chargeback representment process by collecting PMS evidence, analyzing fraud signals with AI, and managing disputes across 66 payment and hotel system integrations.

## Core Capabilities

- Chargeback case management with AI-powered fraud scoring (0-100% confidence)
- **Auto-evidence pipeline**: Matches chargebacks to PMS reservations, collects 7 evidence types, scores confidence, recommends action
- Arbitration workflow for lost cases (filing, document upload, deadline tracking)
- 30 PMS integrations with two-way reservation sync
- 27 dispute processor adapters with portal sign-in and two-way sync (Visa VROL, Mastercom, Ethoca, Verifi, Stripe, Chase, Fiserv, etc.)
- 9 OTA integrations (Booking.com, Expedia, Airbnb, Hotels.com, TripAdvisor, VRBO, Agoda, Priceline, Engine)
- 4 AI agents for evidence collection, response generation, case analysis, deadline monitoring
- Role-based access control (Admin, Manager, Staff) with property-level isolation
- Real-time dashboard with KPIs, analytics, and trend reporting
- Interactive tutorial system and AI-powered ChatHelp assistant
- Demo mode that operates fully without database or Redis (50 endpoints, zero 500 errors)

## Tech Stack

- Backend: Node.js 20 LTS, Express 4.18, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, Redis, BullMQ
- Frontend: React 18, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS 3.4, React Router 6, Recharts, Lucide React
- AI Providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama (configurable; demo uses rule-based scoring)
- Storage: AWS S3 for evidence, local filesystem fallback
- Infrastructure: Docker, Terraform (AWS), Vercel, Render/Railway

## Architecture

### Auto-Evidence Pipeline (Core Feature)

```
Chargeback Received → Match to PMS Reservation → Collect Evidence → Score Confidence → Recommend Action
```

1. **reservationMatcher.js** — 6 matching strategies: exact confirmation# (100%), card+dates (90%), name+dates (75%), card-only (60%), name-only (50%), email (70%)
2. **autoclerkEmulator.js** — In-memory PMS emulator: 10 guests, 13 reservations, 7 evidence types (folio, registration_card, payment_receipt, guest_signature, id_scan, reservation, audit_trail)
3. **fraudDetection.js** — Rule-based scoring: 15 positive factors (+2 to +15 pts) and 10 negative factors (-3 to -20 pts). Base score 50, clamped 0-100. Case-level flags override evidence presence.
4. **autoEvidenceCollector.js** — Pipeline orchestrator: Match → Collect → Score → Notify. Runs synchronously in demo mode.

Score-based recommendations:
- 85-100%: AUTO_SUBMIT (submit defense immediately)
- 70-84%: REVIEW_RECOMMENDED (manual review)
- 50-69%: GATHER_MORE_EVIDENCE (missing documentation)
- 0-49%: UNLIKELY_TO_WIN (consider accepting loss)

### Backend (Port 8000)

Entry point: `backend/server.js` with automatic demo mode detection.

API Route Groups (50 endpoints):
- `/api/auth` — JWT authentication with refresh token rotation
- `/api/cases` — Case CRUD, submit, notes, arbitration, auto-collect pipeline
- `/api/evidence` — Evidence collection (with fraud analysis), upload, summary
- `/api/analytics` — Dashboard KPIs, trend data
- `/api/admin` — Users, properties, agents, settings, audit log
- `/api/disputes` — 27 dispute processor companies
- `/api/notifications` — Notification count
- `/api/pms` — PMS connect, sync, config, disconnect
- `/api/reservations` — Multi-PMS reservation search (?pmsSource=)
- `/api/sync` — Sync status
- `/api/webhooks` — Payment processor webhooks

Key Services:
- `services/autoEvidenceCollector.js` — Pipeline orchestrator (demo mode)
- `services/reservationMatcher.js` — Multi-strategy reservation matching
- `services/fraudDetection.js` — Rule-based confidence scoring (25 factors)
- `services/autoclerkEmulator.js` — In-memory PMS emulator
- `services/aiAgents.js` — AI agent definitions (production: Claude/GPT)
- `services/pmsIntegration.js` — 12 PMS adapter definitions
- `services/disputeCompanies.js` — 27 dispute companies with portal URLs
- `services/queue/workers/` — BullMQ workers (production async pipeline)

### Frontend (Port 3000)

Single-page React app with Vite dev server proxying `/api` to backend.

Pages (12):
- `Login.jsx` — Authentication with demo credentials
- `Dashboard.jsx` — KPI cards (clickable), case breakdown, "Respond to Cases" button (turns red with badge when urgent)
- `Cases.jsx` — Case list with status filters, merged urgent/respond button
- `CaseDetail.jsx` — Timeline, evidence with "Auto-Collect" button, fraud indicators, submit/arbitration, document upload
- `Analytics.jsx` — Charts, trends, performance reports
- `Reservations.jsx` — Multi-PMS tabs, reservation search, evidence collection
- `PMSIntegration.jsx` — 30 PMS systems, portal sign-in, configure, connect/disconnect
- `DisputeIntegration.jsx` — 27 dispute processors, portal sign-in, configure, two-way sync
- `OTAIntegration.jsx` — 9 OTA channels, portal sign-in, configure
- `Settings.jsx` — System config, AI defense, notifications, user management
- `Tutorial.jsx` — Interactive 7-step onboarding
- `Contact.jsx` — Support info

Components:
- `Layout.jsx` — Sidebar, top bar, notification bell, mobile bottom bar
- `ChatHelp.jsx` — AI-powered contextual help assistant
- `ReservationViewer.jsx` — Reservation detail modal with folio
- `GuestFolioViewer.jsx` — Guest folio display

## Integrations (66 Total)

### PMS (30)
Enterprise (15): AutoClerk (built-in emulator), Oracle Opera Cloud, Mews, Cloudbeds, Agilysys, Infor, Stayntouch, RoomKey, Maestro, Hotelogix, RMS Cloud, Protel, eZee, SIHOT, innRoad
Boutique (6): Little Hotelier, Frontdesk Anywhere, WebRezPro, ThinkReservations, ResNexus, Guestline
Vacation Rental (4): Guesty, Hostaway, Lodgify, Escapia
Brand-Specific (5): Marriott GXP, Hilton OnQ, Hyatt Opera, IHG Concerto, Best Western BWR

### Dispute Processors (27)
Prevention Networks (3): Verifi (Visa CDRN/RDR), Ethoca (Mastercard), Merlink( Shift 4)
Hospitality Services (9): StaySettle, Canary Technologies, Chargeback Gurus, ChargebackHelp, Clearview, Chargeblast, Chargebacks911, Midigator, Riskified
Card Network Portals (4): Visa VROL, Mastercom, AMEX Merchant, Discover Dispute
Merchant Processors (9): Chase Merchant, Stripe Disputes, Elavon, Fiserv, Global Payments, TSYS, Square, Authorize.net, Worldpay
Fraud Prevention (2): Kount, Signifyd

### OTA (9)
Booking.com, Expedia, Airbnb, Hotels.com, TripAdvisor, VRBO, Agoda, Priceline, Engine

## Arbitration Workflow

Lost cases may be eligible for arbitration (card network appeal):
- Filing with narrative explanation and supporting documents
- Document upload via drag-and-drop (PDF, DOC, images, spreadsheets)
- Deadline and filing fee tracking ($500 non-refundable)
- Status progression: AVAILABLE -> FILED -> card network binding decision

## Security

- JWT authentication with refresh token rotation and Redis-backed blacklisting
- bcrypt password hashing (12 salt rounds)
- Rate limiting: 100 req/15min general, 20 req/15min auth endpoints
- Webhook signature validation for all payment processors
- Helmet security headers, configurable CORS origins
- Role-based access control with property-level data isolation

## Demo Mode

Fully functional without PostgreSQL or Redis. Activated automatically when database is unavailable or via `DEMO_MODE=true`. Uses in-memory mock data with 9 demo cases, 13 reservations, and pre-configured integrations. All 50 endpoints return meaningful demo data — zero 500 errors.

Demo credentials:
- Admin: admin@disputeai.com / DisputeAdmin123!
- Manager: manager@disputeai.com / DisputeManager123!
- Staff: staff@disputeai.com / DisputeStaff123!

## Quick Start

```
cd backend && npm install && cp .env.example .env && node server.js
cd frontend && npm install && npx vite --port 3000
```

Frontend: http://localhost:3000
Backend API: http://localhost:8000

## Deployment

Docker: `docker-compose up --build`
Development: `docker-compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up`
Frontend: Vercel with included vercel.json
Backend: Dockerfile included for Render/Railway/any container platform
